agent_guidelines: You are a friendly intake assistant. Walk the user through each section conversationally. Ask one or two questions at a time. When the user has to choose from a list, use show_lookup_data to display it. Be efficient — don't repeat questions, and confirm briefly between sections.

completion.confirmation_message: Your request has been submitted. The team will follow up shortly.

description: A small reference example showing all major schema features. Use it to verify the runtime works end-to-end.
helpful_context: This intake form is used by internal teams to submit work requests to the operations group. Most requests are routine and fall under Type A. Time-sensitive items go through Type B (priority) and require manager sign-off; custom or unusual work is Type C. The team aims to acknowledge every submission within one business day and provide an ETA within three.
tips: If the user isn't sure about request_type, suggest comparing the options side-by-side using compare_options.
Target date must be at least 7 days out — gently remind the user if they propose something sooner.

values.label: Type A — Standard
values.description: The default request type. Routine work with no special handling.
values.when_to_choose: Pick this if your request isn't time-sensitive and doesn't require any custom configuration. Most requests fall here.

values.label: Type B — Priority
values.description: Expedited handling for time-sensitive requests. Requires manager sign-off and may incur an expediting fee.
values.when_to_choose: Use only when the timeline genuinely cannot wait — typically 24-48 hour turnaround.

values.label: Type C — Custom
values.description: Bespoke or non-standard requests that don't fit Type A or Type B. The intake team will reach out to scope the work.
values.when_to_choose: Choose this when your request involves unusual constraints, custom integrations, or anything outside normal operations.
examples: Cross-team initiative without an existing template

name: Example Intake Form

fields.label: Your Email
fields.prompt_hint: What email address should we use to contact you?

fields.label: Request Type
fields.prompt_hint: What kind of request is this?
fields.helpful_context: Most requests are Type A. Type B is for genuine urgency and requires manager sign-off; Type C is for anything that doesn't fit a standard pattern.
tips: If you're unsure, ask the assistant to compare the three types side-by-side.

fields.label: Target Date
fields.prompt_hint: When do you need this by? Must be at least a week out.
fields.helpful_context: The team needs at least a week to schedule routine work. Time-sensitive requests should use Type B (Priority) instead of compressing the target date.
common_mistakes: Picking a date in the past
Picking a date less than 7 days out (use Type B priority instead)

fields.label: Description
fields.prompt_hint: Please describe what you need in a few sentences.
